strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded and distributed systems, demonstrating the architectural breadth a CTO role demands
Current Staff Engineer role at re:cinq actively building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks — directly relevant to the agentic AI architecture mandate

Proven fintech and high-stakes systems exposure (RIDE Capital investment platform, Dunnhumby microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second) plus founder experience running an event-driven platform on Kubernetes
concerns: Title progression peaks at Staff Engineer; explicit executive leadership scaling teams from 10 to 30+ is not documented
Multi-agent AI production experience is recent (since April 2025) rather than spanning years of operating such systems at scale
Fintech tenure is meaningful but not centered on credit-risk or working-capital algorithmic systems specifically

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic AI and MCP framework work at re:cinq
Fintech-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital plus founder-level ownership at GISCollective

response.justification: The candidate brings a rare combination directly aligned with this mandate: active production work on agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, deep distributed-systems chops proven at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second, low-latency D services, async I/O, caching), and fintech exposure at RIDE Capital applying TDD, CI/CD and trunk-based discipline — exactly the production-reliability culture the role demands. Founding and operating GISCollective on Kubernetes with an event-driven architecture demonstrates end-to-end ownership, model-agnostic pragmatism ('right tool for the job'), and the ability to ship and sustain real systems. Combined with a long track record of mentoring through workshops, courses and dojos, the profile reads as a hands-on technologist ready to step into a senior architectural leadership seat on agentic fintech infrastructure.
